Title: Dissecting the transcriptional regulation of proanthocyanidin and anthocyanin biosynthesis in soybean (Glycine max)

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Used as cofactor in protoplast assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required cofactor enabling MYB activators to activate PA/anthocyanin target promoters
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmTT8 is required with GmWD40 for GmTT2A, GmTT2B, GmMYB5A, and R to activate target promoters. 2. It is part of the soybean MBW regulatory complex controlling PA/anthocyanin genes.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PAs and anthocyanins are beneficial phytochemicals and pigments.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GmANR1 promoter; GmLAR2 promoter; GmANS promoter; GmGST26 promoter
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential supporting factor in MBW engineering, though not directly manipulated alone in this study.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a primary manipulated gene alone, but essential mechanistic core component.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Used as cofactor in protoplast assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required cofactor for activation of PA/anthocyanin biosynthetic promoters by MYB regulators
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmWD40 acts with GmTT8 to permit GmTT2A, GmTT2B, GmMYB5A, and R to activate target promoters. 2. It is a core component of the MBW complex in soybean.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PAs and anthocyanins are beneficial phytochemicals and pigments.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GmANR1 promoter; GmLAR2 promoter; GmANS promoter; GmGST26 promoter
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential supportive MBW component for pathway engineering.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mechanistic cofactor rather than direct standalone subject of overexpression phenotyping.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Likely vacuolar/transport-related in pigmented hairy roots
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly induced in R-expressing hairy roots; much lower in GmTT2A/B or GmMYB5A lines
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with anthocyanin transport program downstream of R
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmMATE82 is strongly induced in R-overexpressing hairy roots. 2. It is proposed to have a unique role in anthocyanin transport in soybean.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anthocyanins contribute pigmentation and beneficial phytochemicals.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate transporter target for improving anthocyanin sequestration/accumulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Suggested not orthologous to known Medicago anthocyanin MATE transporters, implying soybean-specific transport roles.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Likely vacuolar/transport-related in pigmented hairy roots
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly induced in R-expressing hairy roots; much lower in GmTT2A/B or GmMYB5A lines
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with anthocyanin transport program downstream of R
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmMATE98 is strongly induced in R-overexpressing hairy roots. 2. It is proposed as a soybean anthocyanin transport candidate.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anthocyanins contribute pigmentation and beneficial phytochemicals.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential transporter engineering target for anthocyanin accumulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Together with GmMATE82 may represent soybean-specific anthocyanin transport module.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in GmTT2A-, GmTT2B-, and GmMYB5A-OX lines, but not in R-OX lines
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with PA-specific downstream transport/sequestration functions
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmAHA10 is induced by PA regulators GmTT2A, GmTT2B and GmMYB5A but not by R. 2. This pattern suggests specialized downstream functions in PA rather than anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PAs are beneficial for human and livestock health.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream engineering target for improving PA deposition/sequestration.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as a late biosynthetic/downstream gene regulated by MBW factors in legumes.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Family reported to be expressed exclusively in phloem region
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly induced in GmTT2A- and GmTT2B-OX hairy roots, but not in R-OX lines
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Marks/associates with phloem-localized PA accumulation rather than directly controlling biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmSEOr is strongly induced in GmTT2A/B-overexpressing soybean hairy roots. 2. Its phloem-specific expression is consistent with phloem-localized PA accumulation. 3. Promoter assays indicate it is not a direct target of the GmTT2-GmTT8-GmWD40 complex, suggesting secondary induction.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Not directly regulated by GmTT2A/GmTT8/GmWD40 complex
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PAs are beneficial for human and livestock health.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GmSEOr promoter tested in protoplast assay
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker for vascular/phloem-associated PA deposition patterns rather than a primary engineering target.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely secondary response to PA accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Phloem/vascular tissues of hairy roots; hilum and neighboring seed coat region in seeds
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces anthocyanidins to 2,3-cis-flavan-3-ols such as epicatechin for PA starter/extension units.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key PA pathway enzyme; one of two key reductases in PA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Peaks at early stages of seed development and declines sharply afterward
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in GmTT2A- and GmTT2B-overexpressing hairy roots and GmTT2B transgenic plants; slight induction by GmMYB5A; not detectable in R-OX lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Constitutive 35S-driven TF overexpression in hairy roots and transgenic plants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmANR1 expression pattern parallels GmTT2A/GmTT2B during seed development. 2. Its promoter is strongly activated by GmTT2A or GmTT2B together with GmTT8 and GmWD40, and also by GmMYB5A with these cofactors. 3. Transcript abundance is strongly increased in GmTT2A/GmTT2B-overexpressing soybean hairy roots and in GmTT2B transgenic soybean plants. 4. Differential induction of GmANR1 versus GmLAR2 indicates distinct transcriptional control of PA branch flux in soybean.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes PA accumulation by supplying epicatechin units; dominant PA-associated ANR allele in soybean
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; promoter transactivation assay in Arabidopsis protoplasts; RNA-seq; correlation with PA metabolite profiling in TF-OX hairy roots and plants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GmANR2 was not detectable in the tested lines, indicating GmANR1 is the dominantly expressed ANR allele. In soybean, TT2 homologs preferentially induce ANR over LAR, unlike in Medicago.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful PA branch target to enhance epicatechin-derived PA accumulation, especially in combination with upstream activators such as GmTT2B and anthocyanin precursor-enhancing regulators.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Flux diversion from anthocyanin glycosylation branch is suggested; broader TF overexpression may affect many non-target genes.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PAs are beneficial for human and livestock health.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Phloem/vascular tissues of hairy roots; seed coat/hilum regions in plants
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces leucoanthocyanidins to 2,3-trans-flavan-3-ols such as catechin in the PA pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key PA pathway enzyme; one of two key reductases in PA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Dominant LAR allele during seed development relative to GmLAR1
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Promoter activated by GmTT2A/GmTT2B/GmMYB5A with GmTT8 and GmWD40 in protoplasts, but endogenous expression in soybean hairy roots is induced mainly by GmMYB5A and not by GmTT2A/GmTT2B or GmTT2B transgenic plants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmLAR2 is the dominantly expressed soybean LAR allele. 2. Its promoter can be activated by GmTT2A/GmTT2B or GmMYB5A in the presence of GmTT8 and GmWD40. 3. In vivo, endogenous GmLAR2 transcripts are induced mainly in GmMYB5A-overexpressing hairy roots and not in GmTT2B transgenic plants. 4. This differential regulation versus GmANR1 reveals soybean-specific partitioning of PA branch control.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports PA biosynthesis through catechin formation, with strongest apparent control by GmMYB5A
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; promoter transactivation assay; RNA-seq comparison
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Soybean differs from Medicago, where TT2 homologs strongly induce both ANR and LAR. Here GmLAR2 responds predominantly to GmMYB5A.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for stacking with GmMYB5A or combined regulator strategies to broaden PA monomer composition.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PAs are beneficial for human and livestock health.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Primarily phloem/vascular tissues in soybean hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PAs are beneficial for human and livestock health.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Peaks at early seed development and declines sharply afterward
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ectopically expressed in soybean hairy roots; induces PA-associated transcriptional program
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GmANR1; GmLAR2; PA/anthocyanin late biosynthetic genes including F3H, F3'H, ANS
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates GmANR1 and GmLAR2 promoters strongly with GmTT8 and GmWD40; in soybean hairy roots strongly increases GmANR1 and also elevates anthocyanin-pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: PA biosynthesis and overlapping anthocyanin precursor pathway
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in MBW ternary complex with GmTT8 and GmWD40
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GmTT8; GmWD40; GmMYB170; GmMYB177; GmMYB203; GmMYB211
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Induces repressor ETC1 in hairy roots; broader network effects suggest secondary feedback
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Soybean TT2 homolog that decisively activates PA production, especially epicatechin-based PAs
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmTT2A is a soybean TT2 homolog that complements the Arabidopsis tt2 mutant. 2. Overexpression in Medicago and soybean hairy roots induces PA accumulation. 3. It activates GmANR1 and GmLAR2 promoters with GmTT8/GmWD40, but in soybean preferentially upregulates GmANR1 in vivo. 4. PA accumulation in soybean roots is spatially restricted mainly to phloem tissues.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes PA accumulation in soybean hairy roots, primarily in vascular tissues
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Arabidopsis tt2 complementation; soybean and Medicago hairy root overexpression; DMACA staining; PA quantification; LC-MS/HPLC; protoplast transactivation assay; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 35S-driven ectopic expression in hairy roots; compared across Clark and Williams 82 backgrounds
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression alters expression of >1000 genes, indicating possible off-target developmental/metabolic effects
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising PA-engineering regulator, especially for increasing seed/vascular PA levels, but may need stacking with anthocyanin regulators for precursor supply and with strategies overcoming seed-coat silencing.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Compared with GmTT2B, partially overlapping but non-redundant transcriptional outputs were observed.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Phloem/vascular tissues in hairy roots; hilum and neighboring seed coat; vascular tissues in leaves
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PAs are beneficial for human and livestock health.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Peaks at early seed development and declines sharply afterward
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ectopically expressed in hairy roots and whole plants; high-expression plant lines show PA accumulation in seed coats and leaves
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GmANR1; GmLAR2; PA/anthocyanin late biosynthetic genes including F3H, F3'H, ANS
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates GmANR1 and GmLAR2 promoters with GmTT8/GmWD40; in vivo strongly upregulates GmANR1 but not GmLAR2
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: PA biosynthesis and overlapping anthocyanin precursor pathway
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in MBW ternary complex with GmTT8 and GmWD40
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GmTT8; GmWD40; GmMYB170; GmMYB177; GmMYB203; GmMYB211
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Induces ETC1 and broader transcriptional changes; may trigger secondary phloem-associated responses
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Core soybean PA activator validated in hairy roots and transgenic whole plants
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmTT2B is a TT2 homolog that complements Arabidopsis tt2 and induces PAs in Medicago and soybean hairy roots. 2. In soybean hairy roots and transgenic plants, it preferentially induces GmANR1 rather than GmLAR2. 3. Stable overexpression increases soluble and insoluble PAs in seed coat regions and leaves. 4. PA deposition remains spatially restricted, largely to vascular/hilum-associated tissues.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes PA accumulation in soybean hairy roots, seeds, and leaves
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Arabidopsis tt2 complementation; hairy root overexpression; stable soybean transformation; DMACA staining; PA quantification; LC-MS/HPLC; protoplast transactivation assay; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 35S-driven overexpression in hairy roots and transgenic Williams 82 plants
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Grey hilum phenotype in strong lines; broad transcriptome reprogramming may affect other processes
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lead regulator for engineering soybean PAs; authors propose crossing GmTT2B-OX with MtLAP1-OX to further boost PA levels.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Naturally occurring microRNA mechanism in yellow-seeded soybean may limit spread of seed-coat PA accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: PA pathway regulation in hairy roots; no obvious DMACA-visible PA accumulation reported in whole roots
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PAs are beneficial for human and livestock health.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpression in soybean hairy roots reprograms 1766 upregulated genes and preferentially induces LAR branch expression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GmLAR2; GmANR1; FLS; ANS; AHA10; CHI
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates GmANR1 and GmLAR2 promoters with GmTT8 and GmWD40; in vivo strongly induces GmLAR2 and only slightly induces GmANR1
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: PA biosynthesis branch choice and overlapping flavonoid metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GmTT8; GmWD40; GmMYB170; GmMYB177; GmMYB203; GmMYB211
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Strongly co-induces multiple MYB repressors, suggesting negative-feedback control
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent PA regulator with preferential control of the LAR branch in soybean
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmMYB5A activates PA pathway promoters in the presence of GmTT8 and GmWD40. 2. Overexpression in soybean hairy roots preferentially induces GmLAR2 over GmANR1, unlike the soybean TT2 homologs. 3. It triggers a broad transcriptional response including strong induction of MYB repressors. 4. Despite gene activation, obvious DMACA-positive PA accumulation in soybean hairy roots was not observed under the conditions shown.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Shifts PA pathway gene expression toward the LAR branch and contributes to PA-related transcriptional programming
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protoplast transactivation assay; soybean hairy root overexpression; qRT-PCR; RNA-seq
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 35S-driven ectopic expression in soybean hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Very broad transcriptional changes including many TFs and induction of repressors may constrain net metabolite gain
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stacking partner to diversify PA monomer composition by enhancing LAR branch expression.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Regulatory behavior in soybean differs from Medicago MYB5 responses.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anthocyanins contribute pigmentation and are beneficial phytochemicals.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpression causes red/purple pigmentation in soybean hairy roots; strongly activates anthocyanin program
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Seed coat color genetic background; delphinidin branch absent in W82 due to null F3'5'H allele
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GmANS; anthocyanin genes including F3H, DFR, UFGT, GST, MATE82, MATE98, AOMT, F3'5'H
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates GmANS promoter with GmTT8 and GmWD40; strongly induces anthocyanin biosynthetic and transport genes
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Induces some MYB repressors (notably GmMYB203 and GmMYB211)
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major anthocyanin activator in soybean, especially in colored tissues
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. R activates the GmANS promoter together with GmTT8 and GmWD40. 2. Overexpression in soybean hairy roots causes visible anthocyanin accumulation, with color depending on genotype background. 3. It strongly induces anthocyanin structural, modification, and transport genes including UFGT, GST, and MATEs. 4. It does not induce GmANR1 and therefore favors anthocyanin over PA branch output.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation in soybean hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Soybean hairy root overexpression; visible pigmentation; qRT-PCR; RNA-seq; protoplast transactivation assay; anthocyanin HPLC/LC-MS
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 35S-driven expression in Clark and Williams 82 hairy roots; anthocyanin composition affected by F3'5'H null allele in W82
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad transcriptome changes; can induce overlapping pathway genes and repressors
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful anthocyanin activator and precursor-supply partner for PA engineering, but alone favors anthocyanin branch output.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Black-seed backgrounds can accumulate delphinidin-based anthocyanins, whereas yellow-seed W82 cannot.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves, hypocotyls, roots, embryos, and seed coats of transgenic soybean plants
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anthocyanins contribute pigmentation and beneficial phytochemical properties.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: 35S overexpression in soybean induces strong anthocyanin pigmentation in multiple tissues
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Induces anthocyanin biosynthetic genes including GmDFR2; does not induce GmANR1 or GmLAR2 in soybean plants
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis, indirectly enhancing precursor supply for PA engineering
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely MBW complex involvement inferred from LAP1/R homolog function
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Proven up-regulator of upstream anthocyanin/PA precursor reactions in legumes and effective anthocyanin inducer in soybean
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Stable expression of MtLAP1 in soybean strongly induces anthocyanin accumulation in seeds, leaves, hypocotyls, and roots. 2. It induces anthocyanin enzyme genes such as GmDFR2 but not GmANR1 or GmLAR2. 3. MtLAP1-expressing soybean lines also show increased PA levels in the hilum region. 4. Authors propose crossing MtLAP1-OX with GmTT2B-OX to further elevate PA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation and increases PA-associated precursor supply in transgenic soybean
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable soybean transformation; visible pigmentation; qRT-PCR; anthocyanin quantification; PA quantification
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Compared with soybean hairy-root RNA-seq signatures; study RNA-seq accession PRJNA604625
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong ectopic pigmentation; broader metabolic reprogramming likely
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Recommended stacking partner with GmTT2B for engineering higher PA content in soybean.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Applied-species effect only; source species is Medicago truncatula.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PAs and anthocyanins are beneficial phytochemicals.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB repressor; MtMYB2 homolog with m2 and semi-conserved DIDLN repression motif
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in flowers according to soybean tissue database
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated strongly in GmMYB5A-overexpressing hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MYB activator-driven GmANR1 and GmGST26 promoter activation modules
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Strongly represses GmTT2A-, GmMYB5A-, and R-dependent transactivation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Negative control of PA and anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression/competition within MBW regulatory system
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Proposed to compete with activator MYBs for TT8/bHLH-associated promoter occupancy
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong repressor capable of nearly abolishing GmANR1 promoter activation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmMYB170 is induced in GmMYB5A-overexpressing soybean hairy roots. 2. It strongly suppresses activator-driven GmANR1 and GmGST26 promoter activity in protoplasts. 3. Motif mutagenesis relieves repression, supporting functional repression motifs. 4. Ectopic expression in Arabidopsis reduces anthocyanins and seed PAs.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; protoplast transactivation assays; motif mutagenesis; Arabidopsis overexpression phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Use for tuning may lower desirable flavonoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for knockout/editing to relieve repression and enhance PA/anthocyanin traits.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homologous to MtMYB2 and likely functions in spatial/temporal restriction of flavonoid accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PAs and anthocyanins are beneficial phytochemicals.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in flowers according to soybean tissue database
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly induced in GmMYB5A-OX and moderately in R-OX hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Induced by GmMYB5A and R overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MYB activator-driven GmANR1 and GmGST26 promoter activation modules
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Essentially abolishes GmANR1 promoter activation by GmTT2A complex and represses GmMYB5A and R activities
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Negative control of PA and anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Proposed competition with activator MYBs in MBW system
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong soybean flavonoid repressor that can nearly eliminate activator output
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmMYB203 is strongly induced by GmMYB5A and moderately by R. 2. It strongly represses activator-dependent GmANR1 and GmGST26 promoter activation. 3. Repression motif mutagenesis reduces its inhibitory activity. 4. Arabidopsis overexpression lowers anthocyanin and PA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; protoplast transactivation assays; motif mutagenesis; Arabidopsis overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing/removal could be useful to increase PA/anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike GmMYB170, one motif mutation exception was noted in mutational analysis.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PAs and anthocyanins are beneficial phytochemicals.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB repressor; MtMYB530-like with m2 and EAR motifs
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in seeds according to soybean tissue database
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MYB activator-driven GmANR1 and GmGST26 promoter activation modules
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Reduces GmANR1 promoter activity to ~10% of control; represses GmMYB5A- and R-mediated activation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Negative control of PA and anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely competes within MBW regulatory system
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Potent repressor of PA/anthocyanin activator function
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmMYB177 is upregulated in GmMYB5A-overexpressing hairy roots. 2. It strongly inhibits GmTT2A-mediated activation of GmANR1 and also inhibits GmMYB5A and R outputs. 3. Repression motifs are functionally important by mutational analysis. 4. Arabidopsis overexpression reduces anthocyanin and PA levels.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; transactivation assays; motif mutagenesis; Arabidopsis overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Good candidate repressor gene for knockout/editing to improve flavonoid accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Seed-biased expression suggests relevance for seed PA regulation.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PAs and anthocyanins are beneficial phytochemicals.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB repressor; MtMYB530-like with m2 and EAR motifs
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in seeds according to soybean tissue database
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Moderately induced in R-OX and GmMYB5A-OX hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Induced by GmMYB5A and R overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Partially represses GmTT2A-dependent activation (~50% remaining), but did not inhibit GmMYB5A or R in assays
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Negative regulation of PA biosynthesis, with selective effects
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely acts within MBW-associated regulatory competition
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent, selective repressor of TT2-mediated PA activation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmMYB211 is moderately induced in R- and GmMYB5A-overexpressing hairy roots. 2. It partially inhibits GmTT2A-mediated GmANR1 activation but does not inhibit GmMYB5A or R in the reported assays. 3. Motif mutagenesis produced unusual behavior, further increasing repression rather than relieving it. 4. Arabidopsis overexpression lowers anthocyanins and seed PAs.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a selective repressor of PA/anthocyanin regulatory output
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; transactivation assays; motif mutagenesis; Arabidopsis overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppression of desired flavonoids; atypical mechanism complicates engineering
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible editing target, but mechanism should be clarified first due to noncanonical mutational behavior.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Displayed distinct mechanism from other repressors in mutation assays.
